DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF KENNETH RADER'S MOTION TO QUASH SUN LIFE'S DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS SEEKING THE PRODUCTION OF PRIVATE CREDIT CARD RECORDS IS DENIED. IN THIS LITIGATION PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT HE BECAME DISABLED FROM HIS OCCUPATION AS A CODE ENFORCEMENT OFFICER FOR THE CITY OF BRENTWOOD ON OR ABOUT JULY 9, 2010. SUN LIFE CONTENDS THAT PLAINTIFF'S DISABILITY WAS NOT MEDICALLY DOCUMENTED UNTIL SEPTEMBER OF 2011, AFTER PLAINTIFF'S EMPLOYMENT WITH THE CITY OF BRENTWOOD, AND HIS SUN LIFE COVERAGE, HAD ENDED. PLAINTIFF'S DAY-TO-DAY ACTIVITIES AND FUNCTIONALITY BOTH BEFORE AND AFTER HIS EMPLOYMENT ENDED ARE OF KEY RELEVANCE TO THE ISSUE OF WHEN AND TO WHAT EXTEND PLAINTIFF BECAME DISABLED. PLAINTIFF'S CREDIT CARD RECORDS FROM JANUARY 1, 2008 TO PRESENT PROVIDE DIRECTLY-RELEVANT EVIDENCE OF THOSE ACTIVITIES THAT MAY NOT BE AVAILABLE FROM OTHER SOURCES. THIS ORDER IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF'S RIGHT TO DESIGNATE ANY SUCH RECORDS FOR PROTECTION UNDER THE PROTECTIVE ORDER ENTERED IN THIS CASE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM ERNST HALPERIN, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED. (D302)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Sun Life'S Deposition Subpoenas Seeking The Production Of Private Credit Card Records
Off Calendar Nov-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF KENNETH RADER's Motion To Quash Sun Life'S Deposition Subpoenas Seeking The Production Of Private Credit Card Records is denied. In this litigation Plaintiff alleges that he became disabled from his occupation as a Code Enforcement Officer for the City of Brentwood on or about July 9, 2010. Sun Life contends that Plaintiff's disability was not medically documented until September of 2011, after Plaintiff's employment with the City of Brentwood, and his Sun Life coverage, had ended. Plaintiff's day-to-day activities and functionality both before and after his employment ended are of key relevance to the issue of when and to what extend Plaintiff became disabled. Plaintiff's credit card records from January 1, 2008 to present provide directly-relevant evidence of those activities that may not be available from other sources. This order is without prejudice to Plaintiff's right to designate any such records for protection under the protective order entered in this case. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem Ernst Halperin, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: RYAN OPGENORTH, ESQ., 415.433.8000, FOR PLAINTIFF KENNETH RADER JR.
ANNE MICHELLE BURR, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, 415.703.1403, FOR DAVE JONES.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT DAVE JONES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS Sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to allege that the Insurance Commissioner breached a specific mandatory duty, which the complaint does not now do. (Schwartz v. Poizner (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 572). A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving the defendant's exercise of discretion. Plaintiff also fails to allege facts showing that the defendant abused his discretion by acting arbitrarily. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
